sanskrit: श्री भगवानुवाचलोकेऽस्मिन्द्विविधा निष्ठा पुरा प्रोक्ता मयानघ । ज्ञानयोगेन सांख्यानां कर्मयोगेन योगिनाम् ॥
hindiTranslation: श्रीभगवान् बोले  हे निष्पाप अर्जुन इस मनुष्यलोकमें दो प्रकारसे होनेवाली निष्ठा मेरे द्वारा पहले कही गयी है ।  उनमें ज्ञानियोंकी निष्ठा ज्ञानयोगसे और योगियोंकी निष्ठा कर्मयोगसे होती है ॥
englishTranslation: The divine said: In this world, I have already taught two kinds of steadfast practice: the path of knowledge for the contemplative, and the path of action for the active.
essence: Different natures are steadied by different disciplines.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: There are two valid ways to live steadily: one through understanding, one through action. Different temperaments need different disciplines.
simpleInsight: You do not have to force one spiritual style. Your nature can guide the way that steadies you.
detailedMeaning: Arjuna has just confessed confusion on the battlefield and asked for one clear answer. Krishna begins by saying that human beings are not all built the same: some are fit for knowledge, others for action. This opens the whole teaching on Karma Yoga.
modernRelevance: You are staring at a life decision and want one perfect rule to settle everything. Instead, the answer may be simpler: some people need study and reflection, others need disciplined action.
translationLiteral: श्रीभगवान् (the divine) / उवाच (said) / लोकेऽस्मिन् (in this world) / द्विविधा (twofold) / निष्ठा (steadfast practice) / पुरा (formerly) / प्रोक्ता (taught) / मया (by me) / अनघ (sinless one) / ज्ञानयोगेन (through the yoga of knowledge) / सांख्यानाम् (of the contemplative knowers) / कर्मयोगेन (through the yoga of action) / योगिनाम् (of the yogis)
insight: It shifts the mind from searching for one universal method to recognizing the right path for one’s temperament.

quoteVariants.text: Two paths steady the human being: knowledge for the contemplative, action for the active.
Know or act. Both can steady you.
There are two steady ways: seeing clearly, or acting fully.
